Japanese borrowed many words from German particularly medical words from the study of medicine in Germany by Japanese doctors during the Meiji period (1868-1912). Also common are words related to mountain climbing and skiing, such as gerende for "ski slope". These German words often sound confusingly like the equivalent English word. Is there a similar origin, or is it just a The Japanese language has three different alphabets: Kanji: Ideographic alphabet of Chinese origin. Every character represents an idea or concept. Hiragana: Phonetic alphabet used mostly in combination with kanji. Katakana: Phonetic alphabet used to represent foreign words, places, onomatopoeias, etc. If the word is represented in kanji, the reading section shows how this word is read. Arbeit as a part time job, and other kinds of workers in Japan Contrary to what many English speakers seem to think, English is not the only language from which Japanese imports words and phrases - and often twists the meanings of. Part-time jobs are common among international students and dependent visa holders in Japan to earn extra pocket money. Read on to find a job The word 「アルバイト」 (arubaito) is used in Japan to refer to part-time jobs, often performed by students or people looking for extra income. The etymology of the word traces back to the German "Arbeit," which means "work." This influence began during the Meiji era (1868-1912), when Western culture was being assimilated in Japan, including language and terminology from various Conclusion on Arbeit in Japanese Now that you have learned and understood the common ways of saying Arbeit in Japanese is "アルバイト", it's time to learn how to say Arbeit in Japanese. Translation for 'Arbeit' using the free German-Japanese dictionary by LANGENSCHEIDT -– with examples, synonyms and pronunciation. The Japanese word アルバイト (arbaito), for a part-time job, comes from the German word “arbeit” and simply means “job” or “work.” The shortened version, バイト (baito), is more commonly used nowadays. On average, 65% of Japanese teenagers work part-time jobs, whether for the experience or to earn money for university. How to say Arbeit in Japanese? What's the Japanese translation of Arbeit?
